title: Sacramento

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 702 (PROMYSE Act) will help ensure that the juvenile justice system is centered around the needs of youth and their families, rather than punitive measures.",
    "Community-based youth development services can promote healing and restoration for at-promise youth.",
    "Programs funded under AB 702 must be modeled on healing-centered, restorative, trauma-informed, and positive youth development approaches.",
    "Community-based organizations (CBOs) provide critical support and skills that can prevent recidivism among youth.",
    "At least 95% of JJCPA funds must be allocated to community-based organizations and public agencies that are not law enforcement entities.",
    "Programs such as those provided by Communities United for Restorative Youth Justice (CURYJ) have shown to prevent violence, crime, and further entrenching youth in the criminal justice system.",
    "Mentoring programs, restorative justice programming, and youth civic engagement initiatives are effective in building cultural and historical appreciation of self while establishing confidence in identity."
  ]
}
```
